description: Skin the model to automatically generate face groups.

6.0.reason: Not listed in the official FLAC3D 6.0 command indexes.

7.0.description: Skin the model to automatically generate face groups. “Skinning” refers to automatic face group generation, which occurs as follows: the program looks for contiguous faces and puts them into automatically named groups within the slot named skin. Two faces are not considered contiguous if: the angle between two faces exceeds the break-angle, or if the two faces belong to different groups according to the command’s slot specification. Unless internal is specified, the operation of the command is restricted to external faces. For this command, only zone faces that have no zone on the other side, a hidden zone on the other side, or a mechanically null zone on the other side, are considered surface faces. When use-hidden-zones is used, zone faces within the specified range will be included for consideration, even if they are a part of zones that are currently hidden.

keywords.name: break-angle
keywords.description: Specify the angle that is used to determine face contiguity for inclusion in a skin group. The default break angle is 45 degrees.

keywords.name: internal
keywords.description: Include internal faces when creating face groups. This will assign group names to faces along internal boundaries where the zone group assignments change. The default is off.

keywords.name: slot
keywords.description: Identify the slot that contains the groups to be used for consideration of contiguity. Use of the group logic is described in Groups. If this keyword is omitted, zone group assignments will be ignored and the internal keyword will not do anything. Use the the special any slot assignment to use all slots.

keywords.name: slot-skin
keywords.description: Place the groups created by this command in a slot named s. If this keyword is omitted, the groups will be place in a slot named skin.

keywords.name: use-hidden-zones
keywords.description: Include faces that are on currently hidden zones if they are within the range. By default this values is off.

keywords.name: break-angle
keywords.description: Specify the angle that is used to determine face contiguity for inclusion in a skin group. The default break angle is 45 degrees.

If this keyword is omitted, zone group assignments will be ignored and the internal keyword will not do anything. Use the the special any slot assignment to use all slots.
